name: Crystal Oscillator — Pierce Topology
description: Pierce crystal oscillator using CMOS inverter (74HC04). Y1 sets frequency with high precision. R1 biases inverter in linear region. C1/C2 form feedback network. R2 limits current through crystal.
notes: Most stable simple oscillator topology. Uses one gate of 74HC04. R1 (1MΩ–10MΩ) biases gate as linear amplifier. C1=C2=22pF for most crystals (check datasheet load capacitance). R2 (330Ω–1kΩ) limits drive level — prevents crystal overdrive. Second gate buffers output. Frequency accuracy: ±30ppm typical, ±5ppm with matched load caps.

components.notes: Hex inverter — one gate used as amp, one as buffer
Bias resistor — gate to output feedback
